Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x1EDB "SHADES-0400-1EDB" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $0400 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Shades.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC program to request player setup before the main game loads. However, as the main game code overwrites this program, the following steps were taken to retain it:
1. The initial file loading part of the BASIC program was removed 2. It was modified at line 2200 to replace the existing SYS call to jump back to a new machine code routine at $0DF8 instead when the program is finished 3. The new machine code routine at $0DF8 sets the processor port ($01) to $34 to bank in all of memory and jumps to another new machine code routine at $DB00 4. The 1536 ($0600) bytes of data representing the modified BASIC program and the new routine at $0DF8 was placed back at $0800 5. The data occupying this space ($0800 - $0E00) was moved to $D000 in the dump so that it could be copied back into place after the program is finished 6. In order to to be able to display the loading screen, the screen RAM part of the image ($03E8 bytes) was used to replace the data at $CC00 7. The data occupying this space ($CC00 - $CFE8) was moved to $D600 in the dump so that it could be copied back into place after the loading screen is displayed 8. A new machine code routine was placed at $0400 which sets the correct VICII settings and BASIC pointers in order to display the loading screen and then run the modified BASIC program 9. This routine also disables the RUN/STOP key by modifying the STOP vector by setting $E1 at $0328 10. The new machine code routine at $DB00 copies the data back down from $D000 to $0800 and $D600 to $CC00 12. To handle the fact that processor port ($01) must be set back to $37 to bank all of ROM back in, it copies a small routine to the bottom of the stack ($0100) and executes it to jump to the games entry point at $1EDB
